enforced.mr_name: BOM at Start of SAM File
preconditions: A SAM file that begins with a byte order mark (BOM)
enforced.oracle: A compliant SAM parser must reject or flag the file due to the presence of a BOM at the start of the file, as per the specification rule that SAM files must not begin with a byte order mark.

evidence.quote:
SAM file contents are 7-bit US-ASCII, except for certain field values as individually specified which may contain other Unicode characters encoded in UTF-8.
Alternatively and equivalently, SAM files are encoded in UTF-8 but non-ASCII characters are permitted only within certain field values as explicitly specified in the descriptions of those fields.%
Hence in particular SAM files must not begin with a byte order mark~(BOM) and lines of text are delimited by ASCII line terminator characters only.

enforced.mr_name: INFO field key-value pair ordering invariance
preconditions: The VCF file contains INFO fields with semicolon-separated key-value pairs
enforced.oracle: The biological meaning and interpretation of the INFO field values must remain unchanged after shuffling the order of key-value pairs

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

quarantine.mr_name: Invalid Character in Read Name Field
preconditions: A SAM record with a valid read name field (column 1) that contains an invalid character such as '@'
quarantine.oracle: A compliant SAM parser must reject or flag the record due to the presence of the invalid '@' character in the read name field, as per the specification rule that read names may contain any printable ASCII characters in the range "[!-~]" apart from '@'.

evidence.quote: Query or read names may contain any printable ASCII characters in the range "[!-~]" apart from `"@"', so that SAM alignment lines can be easily distinguished from header lines.

preconditions: The SAM file contains alignment records with POS field values
The POS field represents 1-based genomic coordinates as per SAM specification
quarantine.oracle: The POS field values in SAM alignment records must remain consistent with 1-based coordinate system regardless of CIGAR operation transformations or field reordering, as the POS field is defined as 1-based in the SAM specification

evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — The SAM Format Specification] [1-based coordinate system] A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is one. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a closed interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is $[3,7]$. The SAM, VCF, GFF and Wiggle formats are using the 1-based coordinate system.

[SAM v1 — The SAM Format Specification] [0-based coordinate system] A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is zero. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a half-closed-half-open interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is $[2,7)$. The BAM format uses the 0-based coordinate system.

preconditions: ##fileformat line is present at the start
quarantine.oracle: The biological meaning and validity of the VCF file must remain unchanged after reordering meta-information lines

evidence.quote:
Meta-information lines are optional, but if they are present then they must be completely well-formed.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Note that BCF, the binary counterpart of VCF, requires that all entries are present.
It is recommended to include meta-information lines describing the entries used in the body of the VCF file.

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field structure key-value ordering invariance
preconditions: The VCF file contains ##INFO meta-lines with key-value pairs
quarantine.oracle: The biological meaning and interpretation of the INFO field definitions must remain unchanged after reordering key-value pairs within ##INFO lines

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
